summary: Matched additional character select menu code, primarily replacing placeholder stubs for mnCharSel_8025D1C4 and mnCharSel_802669F4_OnFrame with C implementations. The PR also corrected related type information in CSSKOStar and changed HSD_SisLib_803A6478 to return its destination buffer, enabling the mnCharSel KO-star text path to match. Evidence is limited to the title and diff excerpt; there were no review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Implemented mnCharSel_8025D1C4 instead of leaving it as a decomp placeholder. The function updates KO-star display state for character select entries, including hiding/showing JObjs, positioning the star object, and switching between individual star icons and numeric text for counts of six or more.

Implemented mnCharSel_802669F4_OnFrame instead of leaving it as a decomp placeholder. The function advances mnCharSel frame state, updates the DVD game cache entries for selected characters/colors, handles menu exit flow, decrements a countdown global, and dispatches state-specific transitions including name-entry and menu navigation calls.
Added mnmain.h, mnmainrule.h, and mnnamenew.h includes to mncharsel.c to support the newly matched OnFrame implementation.
Updated mnCharSel_8025D1C4 declaration in mncharsel.h from unknown parameters to an explicit two-int signature, while preserving the excerpted UNK_RET return annotation.
Corrected struct CSSKOStar in src/melee/mn/types.h by changing text from TextGlyphTexture* to HSD_Text* and adding an 0x09 joint2 byte field.
Changed HSD_SisLib_803A6478 in sislib.c and sislib.h from void to u8* and made it return the destination/output pointer after writing the terminating zero byte.
smart_moves: The match used a targeted type correction in CSSKOStar rather than forcing casts throughout mncharsel.c; treating the KO-star text member as HSD_Text* made direct field accesses such as x4D, x0, x30.w, and x5C natural.

Adding CSSKOStar.joint2 exposed the buffer passed to HSD_SisLib_803A6478, which allowed the KO-star numeric text path to be represented with the real struct layout instead of anonymous offset arithmetic.

Changing HSD_SisLib_803A6478 to return u8* let the caller store the returned value in a temporary and pass it to mnCharSel_8025BC20, matching the observed call/return behavior without inventing a wrapper.

The KO-star implementation preserves explicit child/next traversal and NULL-guard-style temporaries around HSD_JObj access, a common matching tactic when compiler codegen is sensitive to expression shape.
The OnFrame implementation kept game-cache update logic close to the original control flow, including separate handling for mnCharSel_804D6CF5 == 1 versus the looped multi-player path.
decomp_lessons: When a matched function wants to use the result of a helper that was previously typed void, re-check the helper signature in the library code; returning the destination buffer from string/SIS copy helpers may be required for matching callsites.
Struct layout fixes can unlock menu matches. In this PR, CSSKOStar needed both the correct HSD_Text* member type and an extra byte field for a SIS/text buffer pointer source.

Character select KO-star rendering has two display modes: fewer than six KO stars use visible/hidden JObj star icons, while six or more uses text generated through sislib and hides the star icon children.
For UI JObj logic, preserving step-by-step traversal through child and next pointers can matter more than simplifying the code, especially around HSD_JObjSetFlags calls.

Menu OnFrame functions often mix rendering/menu state, persistent game-cache updates, audio calls, and scene transitions; matching them may require adding headers from adjacent menu modules rather than forward-declaring everything.
naming_conventions: Existing doldecomp symbol style is preserved for unmatched or address-derived functions: mnCharSel_8025D1C4, mnCharSel_802669F4_OnFrame, HSD_SisLib_803A6478.
Lifecycle callbacks use suffixes such as OnEnter, OnFrame, and OnLeave in mnCharSel names.
Global data symbols retain address-style names such as mnCharSel_804D6CB0, mnCharSel_804D6CF6, and mnCharSel_804A0BD0 when semantic names are not yet established.
Struct fields with unknown meaning remain offset-like, such as x4, xc, x10, x14, x4D, x5C, and joint2, rather than being overnamed.
Local semantic variables appear where evidence is clear, such as css, stars, joint, and i, while compiler-driven temporaries retain names like temp_r31, temp_r30, and var_r0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in mnCharSel_802669F4_OnFrame, indicating stack-shape preservation was needed for the match.
Kept boolean expressions in non-simplified forms such as !(temp_r0 != 0 && temp_r0 != 1), likely to preserve branch structure.
Used explicit switch cases for match_type values in mnCharSel_8025D1C4 to gate KO-star display logic.
Maintained temporary variables for repeated global/struct accesses instead of fully collapsing expressions, likely supporting register allocation and codegen matching.

Used HSD_JObjGetChild and HSD_JObjGetNext calls in the numeric KO-star branch while using direct child/next member traversal with NULL checks in the small-count branch, reflecting the observed compiler/output-sensitive structure.
Adjusted HSD_SisLib_803A6478 return type and implementation so the caller can consume the returned buffer pointer without an artificial cast-only workaround.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text evidence available was the PR title, "mncharsel matches"; no rationale or reviewer intent was included.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or further type CSSKOStar fields joint, joint2, x4, xc, x10, or x14.
Check whether mnCharSel_8025D1C4 return type is later corrected from UNK_RET to void in mncharsel.h.
Search for other callsites of HSD_SisLib_803A6478 to verify whether the u8* return type affected additional matches.
Find subsequent mncharsel PRs that refine mnCharSel_804A0BD0 or the temporary struct asdf used by OnFrame.
Investigate whether game_cache char_id value 0x21 is later named as a none/random/invalid character constant.
